name: Printers
summary: Inkjet, laser, all-in-one printers $50-$800. High ink-lock-in risk category.

evidence.claim: FTC's 2021 'Nixing the Fix' Right-to-Repair report explicitly cites printer-ink tying as a documented anti-competitive practice with Magnuson-Moss implications.
Consumer Reports analysis of inkjet ink cost-per-page economics.
The Verge coverage of the HP Instant Ink firmware-lock class action (Mobasseri v. HP, N.D. Cal. 2020).

criteria.name: type
criteria.notes: EcoTank / tank printers have 10-50× lower cost per page than cartridge inkjets.

criteria.name: wifi_auto_updates_respect_user
criteria.notes: Critical: HP's firmware updates can disable third-party cartridges (E2).

confabulationPatterns.reality: HP Instant Ink and similar bundle a cheap printer with a recurring ink subscription; lifetime cost is typically 3-5× higher than buying a tank printer outright (E1).
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Has the true annual cost including the ink subscription been compared to buying a tank printer?

confabulationPatterns.reality: HP+ subscription requires HP-brand ink only; third-party cartridges are blocked by firmware (E2). This is the fee/ink-subscription-entanglement pack's canonical trigger.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Does the printer require HP+ enrollment? Is third-party ink blocked?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Interpolated dpi — printer places fewer physical droplets than cited. Real-print resolution is lower; photo quality depends on ink formulation and paper more than the cited dpi.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the cited dpi physical or interpolated?

compatibilityQuestions: How many pages per month do you actually print? Occasional users should consider laser (ink doesn't dry); high-volume users should consider EcoTank.
Do you print photos? If not, color inkjet is usually the wrong choice.

repairability.notes: Print-head clogs and roller wear are not economically repairable on consumer inkjets. Laser printers have more serviceable parts.
